source_paper_bibtex: @article{Ahmadi_2025, title={Robust-to-Dynamics Optimization}, volume={50}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2023.0116}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2023.0116}, number={2}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Ahmadi, Amir Ali and Günlük, Oktay}, year={2025}, month=may, pages={965–992} }
context:
Section 4 (Future directions and a broader agenda), page 34

For example, is the problem of testing membership of a point to the feasible set of an R-LD-LP decidable? We have shown that this problem is NP-hard in general and polynomial-time solvable when the spectral radius of the linear map is bounded away from one. It follows from [5] that the general problem is decidable when n \le 3. In higher dimensions, however, decidability is unknown even in the special case where the input polyhedron is a single halfspace.
title: Decidability of feasible-set membership for robust-to-linear-dynamics linear programs
background_latex:
Consider discrete-time linear dynamics on \(\mathbb{R}^n\) given by \(x_{k+1}=Gx_k\), where \(G\in\mathbb{Q}^{n\times n}\) is a rational matrix. Let the constraint set be a (rational) polyhedron \(P=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n: Ax\le b\}\), with \(A\in\mathbb{Q}^{m\times n\), \(b\in\mathbb{Q}^m\), and inequalities interpreted componentwise.

Define the set of \(P\)-invariant initial conditions under \(G\) by
\[
S := \bigcap_{k=0}^{\infty}\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n : G^k x \in P\} 
   = \bigcap_{k=0}^{\infty}\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n : A G^k x \le b\}.
\]
Equivalently, \(x\in S\) if and only if the entire forward orbit \(\{G^k x\}_{k\ge 0}\) stays in \(P\).

The associated decision problem (membership problem) takes as input \((A,b,G,z)\) with \(z\in\mathbb{Q}^n\) and asks whether \(z\in S\). This is the feasibility question underlying robust-to-linear-dynamics linear programs (R-LD-LPs), i.e., linear programs with the additional constraint that iterates under \(G\) remain in \(P\) forever.
problem_definition_latex:
Given rational data \(A\in\mathbb{Q}^{m\times n}\), \(b\in\mathbb{Q}^m\), \(G\in\mathbb{Q}^{n\times n}\), and \(z\in\mathbb{Q}^n\), decide whether
\[
z \in S := \bigcap_{k=0}^{\infty}\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n : A G^k x \le b\}.
\]
Equivalently, decide whether \(AG^k z\le b\) holds for all integers \(k\ge 0\).

Let the constraint set be a (rational) polyhedron \(P=\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n: Ax\le b\}\), with \(A\in\mathbb{Q}^{m\times n\), \(b\in\mathbb{Q}^m\), and inequalities interpreted componentwise.

Define the set of \(P\)-invariant initial conditions under \(G\) by
\[
S := \bigcap_{k=0}^{\infty}\{x\in\mathbb{R}^n : G^k x \in P\}

\]
Equivalently, \(x\in S\) if and only if the entire forward orbit \(\{G^k x\}_{k\ge 0}\) stays in \(P\).

The associated decision problem (membership problem) takes as input \((A,b,G,z)\) with \(z\in\mathbb{Q}^n\) and asks whether \(z\in S\). This is the feasibility question underlying robust-to-linear-dynamics linear programs (R-LD-LPs), i.e., linear programs with the additional constraint that iterates under \(G\) remain in \(P\) forever.

Given rational data \(A\in\mathbb{Q}^{m\times n}\), \(b\in\mathbb{Q}^m\), \(G\in\mathbb{Q}^{n\times n}\), and \(z\in\mathbb{Q}^n\), decide whether

Equivalently, decide whether \(AG^k z\le b\) holds for all integers \(k\ge 0\).
location_in_paper: Section 4 (Future directions and a broader agenda), page 34
open_problem_quote: For example, is the problem of testing membership of a point to the feasible set of an R-LD-LP decidable? We have shown that this problem is NP-hard in general and polynomial-time solvable when the spectral radius of the linear map is bounded away from one. It follows from [5] that the general problem is decidable when n \le 3. In higher dimensions, however, decidability is unknown even in the special case where the input polyhedron is a single halfspace.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The problem is a decidability question over all rational instances, not a claim that can be refuted by a single finite counterexample within a bounded search space. While one can search for hard instances empirically, negative results would require reductions, and positive results would require a terminating decision procedure, neither of which is well supported by brute-force instance search.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: An evolutionary coding loop could explore candidate decision procedures or special-case algorithms and test them on large randomly generated instances by simulating orbits up to increasing horizons, but such testing cannot certify decidability or correctness on all inputs. It could still be useful to discover heuristics, invariants, or reductions to known decidable subcases that may guide theory.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
In the notation of Ahmadi–Günlük’s robust-to-dynamics optimization framework, the membership question asks whether a rational query point \(z\) satisfies the semi-infinite family of linear inequalities \(AG^k z\le b\) for all \(k\ge 0\), i.e., whether \(z\in S:=\bigcap_{k\ge0}G^{-k}P\). The source paper establishes that this feasible set \(S\) is closed, convex, and invariant, but can be non-polyhedral even for rational data (e.g., irrational rotations yielding a disk), and that membership is NP-hard (Theorem 2.1). It also identifies three “barriers” to finite convergence of the natural outer approximations \(S_r:=\bigcap_{k=0}^r G^{-k}P\): \(\rho(G)\ge 1\), unbounded \(P\), or \(0\in\partial P\). When these are removed (Schur stability, bounded \(P\), and \(0\in\operatorname{int}P\)), the paper gives a pseudo-polynomial bound on a finite \(r\) with \(S=S_r\) and develops SDP-based inner approximations aligned with the objective.

The forward-citing literature included here does not resolve the general decidability question for arbitrary rational \(G\) (notably in the marginally stable/unstable regimes that the source paper flags as delicate and related to Skolem/Pisot-type problems). Instead, it advances algorithmic understanding in restricted settings and via certification/approximation. Work on maximal invariant set computation under Schur stability and compact constraints provides finite-termination criteria (often LMI/S-procedure based) that, in the stable regime, effectively yield decision procedures for membership by computing \(O_\infty\) exactly. In parallel, the “safe learning” line of work develops exact conic reformulations for finite-horizon safety (\(T=1,2\)) and, for \(T=\infty\), emphasizes hardness and turns to SOS/SDP inner approximations under stability assumptions—conceptually mirroring the inner-approximation agenda of Ahmadi–Günlük but without addressing exact infinite-horizon membership.

Overall, the state of the art supports: (i) efficient exact membership/feasibility checks in important stable/compact regimes where \(S\) becomes polyhedral after finitely many steps or can be captured by invariant-set computations, and (ii) increasingly powerful convex-optimization-based certificates (ellipsoids, intersections of ellipsoids, piecewise semi-ellipsoids, support-function conditions) that can certify \(z\in S\) (sufficient conditions) or produce tight inner/outer approximations. The main gap remains the general decidability of exact membership for rational \(G,P,z\) in higher dimensions without stability/interiority/boundedness assumptions; this is precisely where connections to deep open problems on linear recurrence positivity/Skolem-type questions suggest that new ideas from logic/number theory and linear dynamical systems will be needed.

sources.title: Computation of the maximal invariant set of discrete-time linear systems subject to a class of non-convex constraints
sources.theorem_or_result: For discrete-time linear systems \,\(x(t+1)=Ax(t)\) with \(A\) Schur stable and compact constraint set \(X=\Omega\setminus\Theta\) where \(\Omega\) is given by quadratic inequalities and \(\Theta\) by “quasi-smooth” nonlinear inequalities admitting quadratic bounds, the paper gives LMI-based stopping criteria (via the S-procedure) that guarantee finite-time termination of an iterative algorithm and exact computation of the maximal constraint-admissible invariant set \(O_\infty=\bigcap_{k\ge 0}\{x: A^k x\in X\}\). In particular, for purely quadratic constraints (\(\Theta=\mathbb R^n\)), Theorem 1 states: there exists finite \(k^*\) such that an LMI quantity \(R_{\max}(k^*)\le 0\) and then \(O_\infty=O_{k^*}\) (the iterate of the standard preimage recursion). For semialgebraic constraints, Proposition 2 shows that after a polynomial lifting to a higher-dimensional linear system with quadratic constraints, the maximal invariant set of the original system equals the projection/pullback of the lifted maximal invariant set.
sources.connection: The open problem asks decidability of membership in \(S=\bigcap_{k\ge 0}\{x: G^k x\in P\}\) for rational \(G\), rational polyhedron \(P\), and rational query point \(z\). This paper studies computation of maximal invariant sets \(O_\infty=\bigcap_{k\ge 0}\{x: A^k x\in X\}\) and provides finite-time algorithms under strong assumptions (notably \(A\) Schur stable and constraints that are quadratic/quasi-smooth or semialgebraic after lifting). In the special case where \(X=P\) is a polyhedron and \(A=G\) is Schur stable, their framework reduces to the classical polyhedral invariant-set recursion which terminates in finite time, yielding a decision procedure for membership of \(z\) in \(S\) in that stable case. However, the open problem has no stability assumption and allows arbitrary rational \(G\); the paper does not address undecidable/hard cases (e.g., unstable or marginally stable dynamics) nor provide a general decidability result for all rational \(G\). Thus it gives partial progress/algorithms for an important restricted regime (stable dynamics, certain constraint classes), but not a full solution.

sources.title: Safely learning dynamical systems
sources.bibtex:

  year         = {2025},

  journal      = {Foundations of Computational Mathematics},
  note         = {Year/authors not provided in the citation analysis block; journal inferred from DOI prefix and title but should be verified.},
}
sources.theorem_or_result:
For linear dynamics with polyhedral safety set \(S=\{x: h_i^T x\le b_i\}\) and uncertainty sets over the unknown matrix \(A_\star\), the paper gives tractable conic representations of robust safety sets. In particular:
- (T=1) Proposition 1: the one-step robust safe set \(\{x\in S: Ax\in S\ \forall A\in U_k\}\) (with \(U_k\) a polyhedron of matrices consistent with data) is exactly the projection of an explicit linear program obtained by LP duality.
- (T=\infty) Theorem 18: for polyhedral \(S\) and polyhedral \(U_k\) consisting of Schur-stable matrices, it constructs SOS/SDP-representable inner approximations \(\tilde S^{\infty}_{k,d}\subseteq S^{\infty}_k:=\bigcap_{A\in U_k}\{x: A^t x\in S\ \forall t\ge0\}\), with \(\tilde S^{\infty}_{k,d}\) full-dimensional for large enough degree \(d\).
sources.connection:
The open problem asks decidability of membership for \(S=\bigcap_{k\ge0}\{x: AG^k x\le b\}\) for a fixed rational matrix \(G\) and rational polyhedron \(P\). This paper does not address decidability/complexity of exact membership for the infinite-horizon polyhedral invariance set for a given \(G\). Instead it:
- treats a related membership/feasibility question for robust safety sets under uncertainty over \(A\), and shows exact LP/SDP formulations for finite horizons \(T=1,2\);
- for \(T=\infty\), explicitly notes intractability: even for singleton \(U_0=\{A\}\), deciding feasibility of \(x\) in the infinite-horizon constraints is NP-hard (citing Ahmadi–Günlük 2024, Thm 2.1), and therefore provides only SOS/SDP inner approximations \(\tilde S^{\infty}_{k,d}\subseteq S^{\infty}_k\).
Thus it provides partial progress in the sense of (i) clarifying hardness of the exact membership problem (NP-hardness) and (ii) giving tractable sufficient conditions/inner approximations for infinite-horizon safety, but it does not resolve decidability of exact membership for rational \(G,P,z\).

sources.title: Safely learning dynamical systems from short trajectories
sources.theorem_or_result: For unknown linear dynamics with polytopic uncertainty set \(U_k\subseteq\mathbb{R}^{n\times n}\) and polyhedral safety set \(S=\{x: h_i^T x\le b_i\}\), the robust one-step safe query problem \(\min_x c^T x\) s.t. \(x\in S\) and \(Ax\in S\ \forall A\in U_k\) admits an exact linear programming reformulation (Theorem 1). For ellipsoidal \(U_k\), the robust two-step safe query problem requiring \(\{x,Ax,A^2x\}\subseteq S\ \forall A\in U_k\) admits an exact semidefinite programming reformulation (Theorem 8).
sources.connection: The open problem asks for decidability of infinite-horizon polyhedral invariance membership: given rational \(G,P,z\), decide whether \(AG^k z\le b\) for all \(k\ge 0\). This paper does not address the infinite-horizon (\(T=\infty\)) membership/decidability question. Instead it gives exact conic (LP/SDP/SOCP) characterizations for finite-horizon safety sets \(S_k^T=\cap_{f\in U_k} S^T(f)\) with \(T\in\{1,2\}\) under uncertainty in the dynamics. It explicitly notes that handling \(T=\infty\) is left for future work, suggesting use of set-invariance tools (Ahmadi–Günlük 2018), but provides no algorithm/decidability result for the infinite intersection \(\cap_{k\ge 0} G^{-k}P\). Thus it is only indirectly related as finite-step robust safety is a truncation/approximation of the invariant-set condition.

sources.title: Piecewise semi-ellipsoidal control invariant sets
sources.theorem_or_result:
The paper develops a convex-programming (semidefinite programming) method to compute control-invariant sets for discrete-time linear (and switched) control systems under polyhedral state constraints, using the template of piecewise semi-ellipsoids. A central characterization is Theorem 2: for the control system 
\[x_{k+1}=Ax_k+Bu_k,\quad x_k\in X,\]
control invariance of a closed convex set \(S\subseteq X\) containing the origin is equivalent to the dual-space inclusion
\[S^* \supseteq A^\top P_{\mathrm{Im}(B)^\perp}^\top\, P_{\mathrm{Im}(B)^\perp}^{-\top} S^*,\]
which leads (via Theorem 3/Program 1) to an SDP feasibility program enforcing invariance and constraints for a piecewise semi-ellipsoidal gauge.
sources.connection: The open problem asks for decidability of membership in the maximal \(P\)-invariant set \(S=\bigcap_{k\ge 0}\{x:AG^k x\le b\}\) for an autonomous linear map \(G\) and polyhedral \(P\). This paper does not address decidability/complexity of the universal condition \(AG^k z\le b\ \forall k\ge 0\), nor does it give an algorithm that decides membership in \(S\) for rational \((A,b,G,z)\). Instead it studies *synthesis/approximation* of (control) invariant sets using convex optimization and duality (support/gauge, polars), primarily for controlled systems \(x^+=Ax+Bu\) (and switched variants). The autonomous case appears only as background (LMIs for ellipsoidal invariance, inequalities like \(g(S,x)\ge g(S,Ax)\)), which is conceptually related to invariance but does not resolve the membership-decidability question for polyhedral constraints.

sources.title: Geometric control of hybrid systems
sources.theorem_or_result:
The paper gives necessary and sufficient conditions for (controlled/weak) invariance of closed convex sets for linear hybrid systems in terms of support functions. In particular, for a linear hybrid algebraic system with mode constraints, weak invariance of convex sets \((S_q)\) is equivalent to the support-function inequalities:
- for each transition \(q\to_\sigma q'\): \(\delta^*(C_\sigma^\top y\mid S_q)\le \delta^*(E_\sigma^\top y\mid S_{q'})\) for all \(y\);
- for each mode \(q\): \(\langle z, C_q x\rangle\le 0\) for all \(z\) and all \(x\in F_{S_q}(E_q^\top z)\) (equivalently, when differentiable, \(\langle z, C_q\nabla\delta^*(E_q^\top z\mid S_q)\rangle\le 0\)).
They specialize these to tractable sufficient/necessary conditions for ellipsoids (LMIs), polynomial support functions (SOS constraints), and piecewise semi-ellipsoids.
sources.connection: The open problem asks decidability of membership in the maximal \(P\)-invariant set \(S=\bigcap_{k\ge0}\{x:AG^k x\le b\}\) for a single rational linear map \(G\) and rational polyhedron \(P\). This paper does not address decidability/complexity of the membership problem \(AG^k z\le b\ \forall k\) nor provide an algorithm that decides it for arbitrary rational \((A,b,G,z)\). Instead, it develops convex-analytic characterizations and computational relaxations for synthesizing controlled invariant sets for hybrid systems using support functions (often via LMIs/SOS). These techniques could be used to compute inner approximations of invariant sets for linear dynamics (including discrete-time linear maps as a special case), which may help certify that a given \(z\) is invariant (sufficient condition), but they do not yield a complete decision procedure for exact membership in \(S\) for polyhedral \(P\).

sources.title: On the convergence of the backward reachable sets of robust controlled invariant sets for discrete-time linear systems
sources.theorem_or_result:
For discrete-time linear systems with control and bounded additive disturbances 
\(x(t+1)=Ax(t)+Bu(t)+Ed(t)\) under convex compact constraints, the paper proves: if a compact convex robust controlled invariant set \(C_0\) satisfies \(C_0\subseteq \operatorname{int}(C_{k_0})\) for some finite \(k_0>0\) (equivalently \(C_0\subseteq \operatorname{int}(C_\infty)\) where \(C_k=\operatorname{Pre}^k(C_0)\)), then \(C_k\) converges to the maximal RCIS \(C_{\max}\) in Hausdorff distance with an exponential rate, i.e. there exist \(N_0\ge 0\), \(N>0\), \(c>0\), \(a\in(0,1)\) such that \(d(C_{N_0+kN},C_{\max})\le c a^k\) for all \(k\ge 0\). The condition \(C_0\subseteq \operatorname{int}(C_{k})\) can be checked for polytopes via an LP.
sources.connection: The open problem asks for decidability of membership in an infinite intersection \(S=\bigcap_{k\ge 0}\{x:AG^k x\le b\}\) for autonomous linear dynamics \(x_{k+1}=Gx_k\) and polyhedral \(P\). This paper does not address decidability/complexity of checking \(AG^k z\le b\) for all \(k\), nor does it provide an algorithm that decides membership in the maximal invariant set for arbitrary rational \(G,A,b,z\). Instead it studies convergence of iterative backward-reachability (Pre) computations for robust controlled invariant sets (with control and disturbances) and gives sufficient conditions ensuring convergence to the maximal RCIS and an exponential convergence rate. In the disturbance-free, no-control special case, its results relate to invariant-set approximation algorithms (inside-out) that could be used to approximate \(S\), but they do not yield a general decision procedure for exact membership.
